comment.body: @Alma I liked when you posted the morning levels a little earlier the other day before the full morning intraday post. The bell rings pretty early in my time zone. I’m getting better set up charting in tv. It takes me a minute to digest the morning report before coffee kicks in.  I’m still working a back breaking side job after market close. So any slightly earlier morning information is greatly appreciated so I can stop missing these opportunities by a minute still getting set up. Now that I’m tracking vol index levels and futures better I’m recognizing regime flips and not panic chasing or hoping. I’m not reversal hopium scalping anymore. Amazing probability analysis as usual. Thank you 🙏 I’m excited to continue be more prepared ahead of time to take advantage of the bigger picture and lean into my moves with eyes wide open and a calm heart. 💚

comment.body: May I offer some observations on the Nvda purchase obligations? Substrate supplies are tight and so is capacity as tsmc. It appears this commitment addresses this tightness as all the fabless companies compete for tsmc space and sub straights to get their chips built. The conclusion one draws from this $95B disclosure all comes down what they believe about future - if one believes the future is bright for Nvda and AI vs if one believes there is a slowdown coming. If the future is bright and margins stay at least flat, the $95B implies an increase in sales when comparing to current year cost of revenue

it is not about belief in bright vs dark future for AI.
it is about overbuilding the infrastructure. I explained it in a loooong post in january that i keep citing over and over  again.

2000- telecommunication and internet
now it's AI...
